response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine IDE development exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI) and strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, testing tooling, open-source libraries), but the core stack requirements are largely unmet. Their career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms; Java appears only peripherally, and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise — the essential qualifications for building enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE tooling.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI, plus authored developer tooling (test runners, assertion libraries)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing workshops and talks
Proven experience with complex, high-scale systems (low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, and only minor Java exposure
Lacks evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge required for JVM language tooling
Recent work is web/full-stack and AI-agent tooling, distant from enterprise framework and application-server domains
